mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month acceleration in industrial production reflects recovering manufacturing utilization, boosting corporate revenue expectations and driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC between INDPRO monthly change and 20-day forward returns is below 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 8.5%, the labor market slack is severe enough to force continued accommodative monetary and fiscal policy, and the market discounts a floor in corporate earnings, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days as policy support dominates.
failure_condition: When UNRATE > 8.5%, S&P 500 60-day forward returns are negative in more than 45% of triggered observations over a rolling 5-year window.

mechanism_narrative: The combination of a federal funds rate below 0.5% and an unemployment rate above 8% signals an extreme macro dislocation where monetary policy is maximally accommodative relative to labor market distress; this policy backstop compresses equity risk premia and drives positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 days.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 0.21% and UNRATE at 9.5% on 2009-06-01
failure_condition: In periods where FEDFUNDS < 0.5% and UNRATE > 8%, the 20-day forward return is negative more than 40% of the time, or the interaction term adds no incremental explanatory power (F-test p > 0.05) over main effects.

mechanism_narrative: Rising retail sales signal consumer demand strength; however, this only translates into positive S&P 500 returns during early-cycle recovery phases when inventory restocking amplifies sales into earnings. Outside this regime, sales growth is already priced in.
failure_condition: During early-cycle recovery periods, RSAFS growth fails to predict positive 20-day returns (t-stat < 1.5) or the hit rate of positive returns is below 55%.

mechanism_narrative: An increase in housing starts reflects recovering residential investment and construction employment, leading to broad economic multiplier effects that drive positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Rank IC between HOUST monthly change and 60-day forward returns is below 0.02 over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Year-over-year deflation in consumer prices signals weak aggregate demand and rising real debt burdens, leading to downward earnings revisions and negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:CPIAUCSL at 214.459 for 2009-06 indicating low inflation
failure_condition: During months with CPIAUCSL YoY < 0%, S&P 500 20-day forward returns are positive more than 50% of the time.

mechanism_narrative: Positive month-over-month change in nonfarm payrolls indicates expanding labor income and consumer spending capacity, leading to positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days as the market prices in demand resilience.
failure_condition: Rank IC between PAYEMS monthly change and 5-day forward returns is below 0.03 over a 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month growth in retail sales and payrolls indicates a coordinated recovery in both spending and income, generating stronger positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 days than either signal alone as expectations of a consumption-driven earnings rebound strengthen.
failure_condition: The joint signal of RSAFS and PAYEMS growth produces a 20-day forward positive return hit rate below 55%, or the interaction term is not statistically significant (p > 0.05) in a two-factor model.
